title: Dal Tadka
description: Creamy toor dal tempered with ghee, garlic and whole spices. A comforting North Indian staple.

preparation.instruction: Wash and soak the toor dal.
tips: Soaking is optional but recommended for creamier dal and faster cooking
subSteps: Rinse toor dal under running water until water runs clear.
Soak in 3 cups water for 30 minutes.
This helps the dal cook faster and become creamier.
Chop tomato into small pieces.
Slit green chillies lengthwise.
Mince garlic cloves finely.
Measure out cumin seeds, red chilli powder, garam masala, and salt.
Keep ghee ready in a small bowl.

steps.instruction: Cook the dal until completely soft and creamy.
tips: Add a small piece of butter while boiling for extra creaminess.
subSteps: Drain soaked dal and transfer to a heavy pot.
Add 3 cups water, turmeric, and 1/2 tsp salt.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium-low.
Cover and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Mash lightly with the back of a ladle until creamy.
Heat ghee in a small pan over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add cumin seeds — they should sizzle and pop within 10 seconds.
Add green chillies and cook 30 seconds.
Add minced garlic and cook until golden, about 1 minute.
Add chopped tomato and cook until completely softened, 3 minutes.
Stir in red chilli powder and garam masala. Cook 30 seconds.

steps.instruction: Pour the tadka over the dal and simmer together.
subSteps: Pour the hot tadka directly over the cooked dal.
Stir to combine and adjust salt.
Simmer together for 3–5 minutes for flavours to meld.
Garnish with fresh coriander.

seo.metaTitle: Dal Tadka Recipe — Authentic Restaurant Style
seo.metaDescription: Make restaurant-style dal tadka at home with this easy step-by-step recipe. Creamy lentils with fragrant ghee tempering.
